children.text: the term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Agency;
the term “Agency” means the Federal Emergency Management Agency;

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives;
the term “public alert and warning system” means the integrated public alert and warning system of the United States described in section 321 of this title;o
the term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Homeland Security; and

the term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any possession of the United States.

children.heading: Integrated public alert and warning system
children.label: b Integrated public alert and warning system
A
children.text: guidance on the categories of public emergencies and appropriate circumstances that warrant an alert and warning from State, Tribal, and local governments using the public alert and warning system;

children.label: i
children.text: the initiation, or prohibition on the initiation, of alerts by a single authorized or unauthorized individual;

children.label: ii
children.text: testing a State, Tribal, or local government incident management and warning tool without accidentally initiating an alert through the public alert and warning system; and

children.label: iii
children.text: steps a State, Tribal, or local government official should take to mitigate the possibility of the issuance of a false alert through the public alert and warning system;

children.label: C
children.text: the standardization, functionality, and interoperability of incident management and warning tools used by State, Tribal, and local governments to notify the public of an emergency through the public alert and warning system;

children.label: D
children.text: the annual training and recertification of emergency management personnel on requirements for originating and transmitting an alert through the public alert and warning system;

children.label: E
children.text: the procedures, protocols, and guidance concerning the protective action plans that State, Tribal, and local governments shall issue to the public following an alert issued under the public alert and warning system;

children.label: F
children.text: the procedures, protocols, and guidance concerning the communications that State, Tribal, and local governments shall issue to the public following a false alert issued under the public alert and warning system;

children.label: G
children.text: a plan by which State, Tribal, and local government officials may, during an emergency, contact each other as well as Federal officials and participants in the Emergency Alert System and the Wireless Emergency Alert System, when appropriate and necessary, by telephone, text message, or other means of communication regarding an alert that has been distributed to the public; and

children.label: H
children.text: any other procedure the Administrator considers appropriate for maintaining the integrity of and providing for public confidence in the public alert and warning system.

children.heading: Coordination with National Advisory Council report
children.label: 2 Coordination with National Advisory Council report
children.text: The Administrator shall ensure that the minimum requirements developed under paragraph (1) do not conflict with recommendations made for improving the public alert and warning system provided in the report submitted by the National Advisory Council under section 2(b)(7)(B) of the Integrated Public Alert and Warning System Modernization Act of 2015 (; ).Public Law 114–143130 Stat. 332

children.label: A
children.text: appropriate personnel from Federal agencies, including the National Institute of Standards and Technology, the Agency, and the Federal Communications Commission;

children.label: B
children.text: representatives of State and local governments and emergency services personnel, who shall be selected from among individuals nominated by national organizations representing those governments and personnel;

children.label: C
children.text: representatives of Federally recognized Indian tribes and national Indian organizations;

children.label: D
children.text: communications service providers;

children.label: E
children.text: vendors, developers, and manufacturers of systems, facilities, equipment, and capabilities for the provision of communications services;

children.label: F
children.text: third-party service bureaus;

children.label: G
children.text: the national organization representing the licensees and permittees of noncommercial broadcast television stations;

children.label: H
children.text: technical experts from the broadcasting industry;

children.label: I
children.text: educators from the Emergency Management Institute; and

children.label: J
children.text: other individuals with technical expertise as the Administrator determines appropriate.

children.heading: Advice to the administrator
children.label: 4 Advice to the administrator
children.text: In accordance with the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.), the Administrator may obtain advice from a single individual or non-consensus advice from each of the several members of a group without invoking that Act.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Incident management and warning tool validation
children.label: c Incident management and warning tool validation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall establish a process to ensure that an incident management and warning tool used by a State, Tribal, or local government to originate and transmit an alert through the public alert and warning system meets the requirements developed by the Administrator under subsection (b)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: the ability to test an incident management and warning tool in the public alert and warning system lab;

children.label: B
children.text: the ability to certify that an incident management and warning tool complies with the applicable cyber frameworks of the Department of Homeland Security and the National Institute of Standards and Technology;

children.label: C
children.text: a process to certify developers of emergency management software; and

children.label: D
children.text: requiring developers to provide the Administrator with a copy of and rights of use for ongoing testing of each version of incident management and warning tool software before the software is first used by a State, Tribal, or local government.

children.heading: Review and update of memoranda of understanding
children.label: d Review and update of memoranda of understanding
children.text: The Administrator shall review the memoranda of understanding between the Agency and State, Tribal, and local governments with respect to the public alert and warning system to ensure that all agreements ensure compliance with the requirements developed by the Administrator under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Future memoranda
children.label: e Future memoranda
children.text: On and after the date that is 60 days after the date on which the Administrator issues the requirements developed under subsection (b)(1), any new memorandum of understanding entered into between the Agency and a State, Tribal, or local government with respect to the public alert and warning system shall comply with those requirements.

children.heading: Missile alert and warning authorities
children.label: f Missile alert and warning authorities

children.heading: Authority
children.label: A Authority
children.text: On and after the date that is 120 days after , the authority to originate an alert warning the public of a missile launch directed against a State using the public alert and warning system shall reside primarily with the Federal Government.December 20, 2019

children.label: i
children.text: it is not feasible for the Federal Government to alert the public of a missile threat against a State; or

children.label: ii
children.text: it is not in the national security interest of the United States for the Federal Government to alert the public of a missile threat against a State.

children.heading: Activation of system
children.label: C Activation of system
children.text: Upon verification of a missile threat, the President, utilizing established authorities, protocols and procedures, may activate the public alert and warning system.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: D Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to change the command and control relationship between entities of the Federal Government with respect to the identification, dissemination, notification, or alerting of information of missile threats against the United States that was in effect on the day before .December 20, 2019

children.heading: Required processes
children.label: 2 Required processes
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Administrator, shall establish a process to promptly notify a State warning point, and any State entities that the Administrator determines appropriate, following the issuance of an alert described in paragraph (1)(A) so the State may take appropriate action to protect the health, safety, and welfare of the residents of the State.

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: 3 Guidance
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Administrator, shall work with the Governor of a State warning point to develop and implement appropriate protective action plans to respond to an alert described in paragraph (1)(A) for that State.

children.label: A
children.text: examine the feasibility of establishing an alert designation under the public alert and warning system that would be used to alert and warn the public of a missile threat while concurrently alerting a State warning point so that a State may activate related protective action plans; and

children.label: i
children.text: the Subcommittee on Homeland Security of the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Subcommittee on Homeland Security of the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: v
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Use of integrated public alert and warning system lab
children.label: g Use of integrated public alert and warning system lab

children.text: develop a program to increase the utilization of the public alert and warning system lab of the Agency by State, Tribal, and local governments to test incident management and warning tools and train emergency management professionals on alert origination protocols and procedures; and

children.label: A
children.text: the impact on utilization of the public alert and warning system lab by State, Tribal, and local governments, with particular attention given to the impact on utilization in rural areas, resulting from the program developed under paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: any further recommendations that the Administrator would make for additional statutory or appropriations authority necessary to increase the utilization of the public alert and warning system lab by State, Tribal, and local governments.

children.heading: Awareness of alerts and warnings
children.label: h Awareness of alerts and warnings

children.text: conduct a review of the National Watch Center and each Regional Watch Center of the Agency; and

children.label: A
children.text: an assessment of the technical capability of the National and Regional Watch Centers described in paragraph (1) to be notified of alerts and warnings issued by a State through the public alert and warning system;

children.label: B
children.text: a determination of which State alerts and warnings the National and Regional Watch Centers described in paragraph (1) should be aware of; and

children.label: C
children.text: recommendations for improving the ability of the National and Regional Watch Centers described in paragraph (1) to receive any State alerts and warnings that the Administrator determines are appropriate.
the circumstances surrounding the false alert;
the content, cause, and population impacted by the false alert; and
any efforts to mitigate any negative impacts of the false alert.
participation rates in the public alert and warning system; and
any efforts to expand alert, warning, and interoperable communications to rural and underserved areas.

children.heading: Timeline for compliance
children.label: k Timeline for compliance
children.text: Each State shall be given a reasonable amount of time to comply with any new rules, regulations, or requirements imposed under this section.
